title: Offline-First Encrypted Document Vault
problem_statement: Human rights lawyers in countries with frequent internet shutdowns lose access to case files stored in cloud services. No tool exists for offline-first encrypted document management that syncs opportunistically when connectivity returns.
proposed_solution: A local-first encrypted document store with conflict-free replicated data types (CRDTs) for opportunistic sync. Documents encrypted at rest with user-held keys. Sync occurs over any available channel — internet, mesh, Bluetooth, USB.
mvp_scope: Desktop app (Electron or Tauri) with local AES-256 encrypted storage and USB-based sync between devices. Basic folder structure and search.
threat_model: State adversary seizing devices and cutting internet. Cloud providers complying with government data requests.
open_questions: How to handle key recovery without a trusted server?
Maximum practical document size for mesh sync?
